title: be going to (do)
desc: 계획된 의도 또는 근거 있는 예측
examples: I think the coolest thing about studying from Gaelic Spellux is those expressions pop into my head when I am in a similar situation or place Whenever I go to the gym gym-related praise it's just come to mind It's kind of weird Let's hit the gym Everybody work out Things like that It's working, it's working I don't try to study I just try to acquire If you study a chapter then you can follow that Go to the gym and go to the gym That's why I use it And when you go to the gym and you go to the gym like this If you think that you're going to get hurt you can go faster I always do that If you study at home you can't go to the gym It's like a mat practice it So do you have any advice for other people?

I'm going to go to the gym right now So keep going and studying and you can improve your English Don't give up Don't give up guys He's proof How long per day do you spend studying English or practicing English?

I'm going to show you morning routine The very first thing I do things like that I love memorizing Very nice I'm curious What's your favorite content so far And in the future, what kind of content do you want to see?

I love weight training So I just want to teach someone Weight training I'm going to go to the gym again He's telling me to get back in the gym Thank you so much for your time today And I really enjoyed listening to your story Thanks for having me

title: can / could / be able to
examples: I couldn't say a single word in English 공부하게 된 지는 1년쯤 됐고 해장편을 공부하고 해장을 실제로 가야 해요 이제 하고나 안 나가기 시작했죠 갑자기 제가 표현할 수 있는 그 range가 커졌다는 게 좀 느껴졌어요 그때 좀 신기하더라고요 Maybe you can introduce yourself first Hello my name is Jeong In-Gyo You can call me Zachary I'm 33 years old and I run my own brand where I can share a message about the journey of rediscovering who I am How you tell me about your English learning journey You might find it hard to believe but I couldn't say a single word in English before going to the UK one years ago I went to the UK for a missionary work but I couldn't say anything but I just chatted with homeless people who had a really strong British accent and the point is they never judged me by my English level and they always were patient so not only did my English improve but I also lost all of my fear and pressure That's amazing I always froze But now you're completely foolish I say Yes, thanks to you to study with me to study but I tend to forget them in a few days and also I just am afraid of other people's judgments I had negative thoughts in my mind I don't have any talents in English that's real problem It's really cool that you took this problem and you really took action Yeah, took action Why am I afraid of English?

I just reached the homeless people I just talked with them I think perfectionism is such a big hurdle to becoming good at anything in life Yes, yes If you overcome that kind of judgment or fear I think anyone can get good at English I feel like part of it is just in our head too Probably it was my insecurity In Korea, English education is often focused on the test memorized and picked the right answer as fast as possible rather than speaking That's the problem If we study for about a year even if we can get a little closer to each other even if we can communicate with each other I can be confident But I have to be confident But if I want to be confident I can't do it, I can't do it, I can't do it I can't do it I would have been good if I could have done it but I think I should just leave these thoughts and just focus on it I think I did a good job and I did a good job I think I did a good job First of all, I was so happy to tell you I can't be burdened I always go to a place I always go to the mart I go to the gym and I go to the dinner room I think it's almost the same That's why I was very helpful But when I went to the academy I only spoke the same way How are you?

I just do this That's why I can't feel English because I can't go to the academy There's a big academy in Gangnam I was in the first year and I met two weeks ago I think it's important to keep in mind I think I can keep this in mind I think I'm always thinking about it What I thought about is that if I go to a place that's not what I think Is it called the English-speaking word?

It's a word that's called English It's a word that's called English-speaking word It's a word that's called English-speaking word When I was doing that I suddenly felt that I could express it that range was a bit bigger It was a bit interesting I used to do it three times I used to call it V-log and then I learned from A to A and then I learned in English I think A and A are really good I was really surprised when I saw your videos The method of learning that you use is perfect It's amazing I was really inspired watching your videos So I was wondering if you could just tell me how you use the materials?
